behaviors.description: Cron parser rejects invalid expressions with descriptive error messages.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is parser validation and error construction logic. No real I/O, OS behavior, or external service is required to expose invalid-field and malformed-syntax bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven parser test over invalid cron strings (e.g., month=13, day=32, malformed tokens). Assert parse failure, stable error type/code, and message includes offending field/value and expected range/syntax.

behaviors.description: Next-run-time calculator returns correct timestamps, including last-day-of-month and nearest-weekday cases.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily calendar-computation logic from input schedule + start timestamp to deterministic outputs. Real failures can be caught at the algorithm boundary without cross-module infra.
behaviors.test_description: Deterministic date-math tests for representative cron expressions, asserting exact next N timestamps from fixed start instants, including end-of-month rollover, leap-year month ends, and nearest-weekday adjustments.

behaviors.description: Overlap detector finds schedules firing within minimum gap and reports conflicting windows.
behaviors.justification: The real failure often appears at module handoff: run-time generation feeding overlap logic plus gap policy interpretation. Testing only one function can miss boundary/ordering/window-format issues.
behaviors.test_description: Wire parser/schedule calculator with overlap detector. Create two cron schedules whose generated runs are just inside/outside a 5-minute gap; assert detected conflicts and exact reported window boundaries for each collision.

behaviors.description: Timezone-aware scheduling handles DST spring-forward gap and fall-back ambiguity for a 2:30 AM job.
behaviors.justification: To catch real DST failures, the test should exercise real timezone rule behavior (tz database/runtime datetime engine), which is effectively OS/runtime infrastructure. Mocked timezone logic can hide true transition bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Run scheduler with real timezone support (e.g., America/New_York) over concrete DST dates. Verify how nonexistent 2:30 AM is handled on spring-forward and which occurrence(s) are chosen on fall-back, matching product policy.

behaviors.description: Engine applies missed-run policy (skip vs backfill) when computed next run is already in the past.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on interaction between next-run computation, persisted/reference time, and policy module. Single-function tests can miss decision errors caused by state and module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with scheduler state + policy config: simulate downtime window so next run is in the past, then resume engine. Assert skip policy advances to future run and backfill policy emits expected catch-up runs with correct limits/order.
